reasoning: Mathlib's `Sylow p G` encodes "maximal p-subgroup", matching the informal notion of a p-Sylow subgroup. The Lean signature takes a prime p (`hp : Nat.Prime p`), a Sylow p-subgroup P with P.Normal (encoding P ◁ G), and concludes ∀ Q : Sylow p G, P = Q, which is exactly "P is the unique Sylow p-subgroup". Although the Lean statement omits an explicit finiteness hypothesis that Herstein's context implicitly assumes, the statement "a normal maximal p-subgroup is the unique Sylow p-subgroup" is in fact true for arbitrary groups (via PQ/P ≅ Q/(P∩Q) plus maximality of P), so the omission doesn't strengthen the claim beyond what the informal statement asserts. Hypotheses, conclusion, and quantifier structure all match.
